scope.purpose: Pre-register qualitative multi-seed replication criteria for the Direction-A spine before running seed > 0.

arm_a.criterion: Arm A remains approximately zero interior robust violations.
arm_a.interpretation: Uniform random may find moderate or saturated violations, but should continue to miss the thin interior target at this budget.

arm_c_vs_arm_b.criterion: Arm C interior robust-violation count is directionally and materially larger than Arm B, and Arm C clean trigger supports remain in the rough 4-8 range.
arm_c_vs_arm_b.interpretation: The comparison is qualitative and order-of-magnitude, not exact point-estimate matching to seed 0.

arm_c_channels.criterion: Arm C interior robust violations use only the relevant channel set {roll,pitch}.
arm_c_channels.interpretation: Single-channel roll or pitch triggers are acceptable because they are subsets of the pre-registered relevant channel set.

ddmin_necessity.criterion: ddmin remains a failure relative to Arm C: clean yield is lower than Arm C's explainable output, final support median is materially higher than Arm C's clean-support range, and J=5 cost per distinct clean trigger is materially higher.
ddmin_necessity.interpretation: A seed is a deviation if ddmin reliably produces mostly clean, low-support roll/pitch-only triggers at cost comparable to Arm C.

distinct_trigger_cost.criterion: Cost is reported per distinct clean trigger signature, where a signature discretizes active channel set, active time band, and envelope shape.
distinct_trigger_cost.interpretation: This replaces a single amortized seed-0 cost ratio with a per-seed distribution across seeds 0/1/2.

non_tuning_statement: No pre-registered thresholds, property definitions, feasible limits, or CADET five-stage logic may be adjusted to make seed 1 or seed 2 match seed 0.
